rulings.Case Title: MILENA MAIONCHI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 13, 2019 line 1. Haines on file. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: IBRAHIM KAILEH ET AL VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESEDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 13, 2019 line 2. PETITIONER IBRAHIM KAILEH, MAHA KAILEH Re-Notice Of Hearing Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate DENIED. The Court finds that the appellate case Drolapas is good law and it binds this Court by its interpretation of Civil Code § 1954.53(d)(2). The Court further finds that a review of 1954.53(d) case law and its legislative history indicates that the term "reside" is used in a colloquial, ordinary sense. Moreover, the ALJ's decision is both consistent with a colloquial definition of "reside" and reasonably supported by the facts. Therefore, Morgan is a protected lawful sublessee, and he resided at the Unit before January 1, 1996, pursuant to 1954.53(d)(2). The Rent Board did not abuse its discretion in its determination. Any legal or factual dispute concerning Rule 6.14 is moot. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: OLUSOJI FANOIKI VS. 1081-1087 MISSISSIPPI STREET ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Mpa In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Prohibit Foreclosure Sale
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 13, 2019 line 13. PLAINTIFF OLUSOJI FANOIKI Notice And Mpa In Support Of Plaintiffs Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Prohibit Foreclosure Sale GRANTED. Plaintiff shall post with the Court cash or bond in the amount of $11,500 no later than February 13, 2019 by 2:00 p.m. Plaintiff shall also pay all regular and special assessments due and owing (excluding those in dispute on this motion). If any assessment payments are currently past due, the payment shall be made by 2:00 p.m. on February 13, 2019. All future regular and special assessments to be paid in a timely fashion for the duration of the injunction. Injunction shall take effect upon timely posting of the bond and timely payment of the due and owing assessment (to the exclusion of those specified in the joint brief). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BRENT MARSH VS. MICHAEL CAMPESINO ET AL

rulings.Case Title: BRENT MARSH VS. MICHAEL CAMPESINO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 13, 2019 line 14. DEFENDANT MICHAEL CAMPESINO DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiff shall allege facts showing that the Lease agreement with the term starting on November 1, 2018 was properly rescinded under the law; and that right to possession/possession may be recovered without procedural requirements under the State Law (e.g. by changing the locks) if tenant does not occupy the preemies as his primary residence, does not have all of his personal possessions in the unit, and does not sleep in the unit at the time of the rescission. Assuming that the November 7, 2018 rescission (and basis for it) is properly alleged, Plaintiff shall allege facts showing the continued validity of the rescission in light of November 15, 2018 Notice demanding defendant's compliance with his obligations as a tenant under the Lease (specifically, if the lease was rescinded on November 7, 2018 why is the tenant required to comply with it on November 15, 2018). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PRIYA SANGER ET AL VS. LEAH AHN
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 13, 2019 line 11. DEFENDANT LEAH AHN DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with final leave to amend. The Court has previously granted leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts establishing the factors addressed in Rossetto v. Barross (2001) 90 Cal.App.4th Supp. 1, 5 (e.g defendant is in exclusive possession of entire property owned by the tenants in common; the agreement contains the term "subleasing;" the agreement is not recorded; the parties waived partition, etc.) Plaintiff failed to do so. If Plaintiff can point to any other authority (unlawful detainer cases involving tenants in common) Plaintiff may cite those at the hearing (citation to be provided to the defendant's counsel between 3 and 4 p.m. on February 11, 2019). Vucinich v. Gordon (1942) 51 Cal. App. 2d 434 is factually distinguishable. The Court also notes that while the original complaint expressly states that Defendant is not a tenant and that Plaintiff is not a landlord, First Amended Complaint indicates that Defendant is a tenant, but does not allege who the landlord is. Plaintiff shall explain this discrepancy in any amended complaint. Further, if Defendant is a tenant, there is no allegation in the complaint regarding compliance with or exemption from SFRO. All amendments in the amended complaint shall be in bold font. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: LIM'S FAMILY BENEVOLENT ASSOCIATION VS. ROBERT KINANN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 13, 2019 line 12. DEFENDANT ROBERT KINANN Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service OFF CALENDAR. Defendant appears to have filed for bankruptcy. While no proper Notice of Bankruptcy Stay has been filed, a copy of Notice of Bankruptcy Case Filing is attached to Defendant's papers. This action to resume after bankruptcy is resolved or Plaintiff obtains an order lifting the stay. Plaintiff to provide notice to the Court and to the Defendant at the address provided on Defendant's motion to quash of resolution (discharge, dismissal, order lifting stay, etc). = (501/CFH)
